title: Tender Bitter Greek Lamb Stew
description: A hearty, bitter, and tender Greek lamb stew featuring sumac, perfect for a brunch mood. This dish is FODMAP-friendly and slow-cooked to perfection.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes. Remove the browned lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic-infused oil, sumac, dried oregano, and bay leaves to the pot. Cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the browned lamb back to the pot, along with the lemon juice and chicken broth.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Add the chopped radicchio to the pot and continue to cook for an additional 30 minutes, or until the radicchio has wilted.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
